heading: Performance standards to protect against portable fuel container explosions near open flames or other ignition sources
label: 2056d Performance standards to protect against portable fuel container explosions near open flames or other ignition sources

children.heading: Short title
children.label: a Short title
children.text: This section may be cited as the “Portable Fuel Container Safety Act of 2020”.

children.heading: Rule on safety performance standards required
children.label: 1 Rule on safety performance standards required
children.text: Not later than 30 months after , the Consumer Product Safety Commission (referred to in this section as the “Commission”) shall promulgate a final rule to require flame mitigation devices in portable fuel containers that impede the propagation of flame into the container, except as provided in paragraph (3).December 27, 2020

children.label: A
children.text: shall be promulgated in accordance with ; andsection 553 of title 5

children.label: B
children.text: shall be treated as a consumer product safety rule promulgated under .section 2058 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: there is a voluntary standard for flame mitigation devices for those containers that impedes the propagation of flame into the container;

children.label: ii
children.text: the voluntary standard described in clause (i) is or will be in effect not later than 18 months after ; andDecember 27, 2020

children.label: iii
children.text: the voluntary standard described in clause (i) is developed by ASTM International or such other standard development organization that the Commission determines to have met the intent of this section.

children.heading: Determination required to be published in the Federal Register
children.label: B Determination required to be published in the Federal Register
children.text: Any determination made by the Commission under this subsection shall be published in the Federal Register.

children.heading: Treatment of voluntary standard for purpose of enforcement
children.label: 4 Treatment of voluntary standard for purpose of enforcement
A
children.text: 180 days after publication of the Commission’s determination under paragraph (3); or

children.label: B
children.text: the effective date contained in the voluntary standard.

children.heading: Notice to commission
children.label: A Notice to commission
children.text: If the requirements of a voluntary standard that meet the conditions of paragraph (3) are subsequently revised, the organization that revised the standard shall notify the Commission after the final approval of the revision.

children.heading: Effective date of revision
children.label: B Effective date of revision
children.text: Not later than 180 days after the Commission is notified of a revised voluntary standard described in subparagraph (A) (or such later date as the Commission determines appropriate), such revised voluntary standard shall become enforceable as a consumer product safety rule promulgated under , in place of the prior version, unless within 90 days after receiving the notice the Commission determines that the revised voluntary standard does not meet the requirements described in paragraph (3).section 2058 of this title

children.heading: Future rulemaking
children.label: 6 Future rulemaking
children.text: The Commission, at any time after publication of the consumer product safety rule required by paragraph (1), a voluntary standard is treated as a consumer product safety rule under paragraph (4), or a revision is enforceable as a consumer product safety rule under paragraph (5) may initiate a rulemaking in accordance with to modify the requirements or to include any additional provision that the Commission determines is reasonably necessary to protect the public against flame jetting from a portable fuel container. Any rule promulgated under this subsection shall be treated as a consumer product safety rule promulgated under .1section 553 of title 5section 2058 of this titleSo in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.1

children.heading: Education campaign
children.label: A Education campaign
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Commission shall undertake a campaign to educate consumers about the dangers associated with using or storing portable fuel containers for flammable liquids near an open flame or any other source of ignition.December 27, 2020

children.heading: Summary of actions
children.label: B Summary of actions
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Commission shall submit to Congress a summary of actions taken by the Commission in such campaign.December 27, 2020

children.label: A
children.text: intended for flammable liquid fuels with a flash point less than 140 degrees Fahrenheit, including gasoline, kerosene, diesel, ethanol, methanol, denatured alcohol, or biofuels;

children.label: B
children.text: that is a consumer product with a capacity of 5 gallons or less; and

children.label: C
children.text: that the manufacturer knows or reasonably should know is used by consumers for transporting, storing, and dispensing flammable liquid fuels.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 9 Rule of construction
children.text: This section may not be interpreted to conflict with the Children’s Gasoline Burn Prevention Act (; ).Public Law 110–278122 Stat. 2602

children.heading: Children’s Gasoline Burn Prevention Act
children.label: c Children’s Gasoline Burn Prevention Act

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: 2 Applicability
children.text: The amendment made by paragraph (1) shall take effect 6 months after .December 27, 2020
